mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing, reducing discount rates for equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in payroll employment above 130,000 new jobs per month signals strong labor market demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain negative when PAYEMS exceeds 130,000 for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in housing starts and industrial production signals weak aggregate demand, leading to S&P 500 underperformance over 60 trading days as recession risks rise.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive when both HOUST and INDPRO decline for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in industrial production above 97.5 signals robust supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through improved corporate margins.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative when INDPRO exceeds 97.5 for 5 consecutive days.

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day moving average of CPI above 2.5% indicates persistent inflation, prompting central bank rate hikes that depress S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive when CPIAUCSL remains above 2.5% for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in unemployment rate and rise in real sales (RSAFS) indicates tight labor markets and strong consumer spending, driving S&P 500 positive returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative when UNRATE declines and RSAFS increases for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth below 2% signals weak economic momentum, leading to S&P 500 underperformance over 60 trading days as investor risk appetite declines.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive when GDPC1 remains below 2% for 3 consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production combined with rising unemployment indicates supply-side deterioration and weak demand, causing S&P 500 negative returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive when both INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises for 3 consecutive months.
